commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
feat(ai): implement PDF editing via AI chat

Users can now ask the AI to edit/modify an uploaded PDF document:

- ai_upload_file: cache original PDF bytes in Django cache (15 min TTL)
  with a file_id returned to the frontend
- detect_edit_request(): detects editing keywords (cambia, edita, modifica,
  reemplaza, genera uno igual, etc.) — only triggers when PDF has file_id
- _apply_pdf_edit(): helper that calls AI to generate JSON replacement pairs,
  applies them via PyMuPDF (redact + reinsert), stores result in cache
  - Fallback regex parser when AI unavailable (handles DNI/number pairs,
    quoted strings, "X por Y" patterns)
- ai_download_edited(): GET endpoint to download cached PDF edit result
- URL: /asistencia/ia/documento-editado/?id=<edit_id>
- JS: file_id passed in file_context; [DOWNLOAD:pdf_edit:id] SSE marker;
  window.aiDownloadPdf() function; red-styled PDF download button
- CSS: .ai-download-pdf-btn (red gradient to distinguish from Excel)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v30.5 — hovered switch lifts to SVG front, above fans and other elements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use \" | \" as between-drug separator to prevent MoA misalignment

Root cause: some drugs have multiple mechanisms joined by \"; \"
(e.g. Cobimetinib = \"MEK1 inhibitor; MEK2 inhibitor\"). When all drugs'
MoAs were joined with the same \"; \" separator, Cobimetinib's two
mechanisms looked like they belonged to two separate drugs, shifting
every subsequent drug's MoA one position to the right.

Fix: use \" | \" as the between-drug separator for all positional
multi-drug fields. \"; \" now unambiguously means \"multiple values
within one drug\", while \" | \" means \"next drug in the trial\".

Changes:
- models/trial.py: intervention_name and intervention_type now joined
  with \" | \" instead of \"; \"
- enrichment/pipeline.py: _split_names() splits on \"|\" instead of
  \";\"; trial.moa, trial.drug_class, trial.drug_name_normalized joined
  with \" | \" instead of \"; \"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

harden execution engine: MEV protection, quote freshness, exact approvals

- brain.py: reusable Anthropic client, robust regex JSON parsing with fallback
- lifi.py: exact-amount approvals (not MAX_UINT256), quote staleness guard,
  _parse_int for hex/decimal, nonce with "pending" to prevent collisions
- marco.py: pass current_pool to brain, re-fetch fresh quote before execution
  in LIVE mode, validate fresh quote cost before proceeding
- wallet.py: expanded USDC addresses to 11 chains
- yield_scanner.py: validate DefiLlama response structure

commit.message:
Fix users remove to match bare GitHub usernames, add rm alias

Users added via GitHub username are stored as "https://github.com/<name>"
but `users remove` only matched against the full stored name or public key.
Now also matches against the display name (bare username).

- Add "rm" alias for the "remove" subcommand
- Prevent unknown subcommands from silently falling through to list
- Add lifecycle test covering add, remove (can't decrypt), re-add (can decrypt)
- Add regression tests for removal by username, display name, and number

commit.message:
Major enhancement: interactive assumptions, all-doc source viewer, tax bracket chart, Stripe prep

- Source document viewer now works for ALL doc types (1099-INT, 1099-DIV, 1099-NEC, 1099-B, 1098), not just W-2s
- All line items with source data are clickable to view original documents
- Per-employer W-2 wage and withholding breakdown in return view
- Interactive assumption answering: filing status picker, dependent entry, HSA/IRA/student loan deductions, education credits, EITC, business expenses, itemized deductions — all recalculate the return instantly
- Tax bracket visualization showing how income is taxed across brackets
- New "Documents" tab showing all uploaded documents with click-to-view
- Child Tax Credit ($2,000/child) and Other Dependent Credit ($500) support
- Above-the-line deductions: HSA, IRA, student loan interest
- Impact toast notification showing refund change after each refinement
- Comparison table (vs TurboTax vs CPA) on pricing page
- Print button and print-friendly CSS styles
- Version number displayed in top-right corner
- Stripe.js loaded and configured (awaiting publishable key)
- Better mobile responsive styles
- Proper memory cleanup for all doc type object URLs

[DOCS] i18n & Timezone: refonte et clarifications complètes

- i18n: scope CEMAC documenté (FR/EN Phase 1, AR/ES hors scope), tableau langue par contexte (SMS/notif/invitation/PDF), règle invitations sans compte = langue association, "Diaspora hors CEMAC" au lieu de "hors Cameroun"
- Timezone: fusion des 2 sections redondantes en une section complète
- Timezone: start_time/end_time saisis dans timezone association, retournés HH:mm:ss local (non UTC)
- Timezone: jobs planifiés documentés (CloseOverdueSeances/CheckLoanDefaults/OpenDueSessions calculent leurs seuils dans timezone association)
- Timezone: règle précise CheckLoanDefaults (jours calendaires depuis 23h59 due_date timezone association)
- Timezone: abréviation SMS dérivée dynamiquement depuis IANA (pas constante "WAT")
- DATABASE: ajout clé système language (association_settings)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add OpenClaw exec provider protocol support to secretref-wrapper

The wrapper now auto-detects and handles three input protocols:
1. CLI arg (legacy): secretref-wrapper <secret-name>
2. Stdin JSON (legacy): {"secret_name": "..."}
3. OpenClaw exec (new): {"protocolVersion": 1, "ids": [...]}

The OpenClaw protocol supports batch resolution with per-id error
reporting, enabling direct use as an exec SecretRef provider without
a bridge script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

辩前实时研究：每场辩论自动获取辩题最新背景资料

- 新增 lib/research.ts：辩论开始前自动让 AI 分析辩题相关的
  最新数据、新闻动态、正反方素材、冷门角度和经典引用
- 研究结果注入每位辩手的上下文，让论据更具体、更有时效性
- 内存缓存：同一辩题只研究一次，不重复调用
- 零额外成本：复用现有 GLM API，无需第三方搜索服务

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement Phase 4: True Dynamic FSM-LLM Architecture

- Restructure YAML: states as list with extract_variables per state;
  transitions use condition_string instead of condition/next_state
- Rewrite FSM with transitions library: FlowController + dynamic condition
  closures via setattr (works around transitions 0.9.x kwarg restrictions);
  evaluate() / fire_intent() API; simpleeval uses subscript access to avoid
  {} literal restriction
- Add after_model_callback: parses ```json``` block from LLM text,
  deep-merges into ledger, advances FSM — LLM now bridges semantic gap
  between tool outputs and ledger fields
- Remove TOOL_LEDGER_MAP: after_tool handles only detect_intent
- Update before_model: injects extract_variables into system prompt
- Rewrite test_agent_flow.py: TestAfterModelLedgerAndFSM replaces
  TestAfterToolLedgerAndFSM; happy path uses run_after_model()
- All 58 tests pass (20 FSM unit + 38 agent flow integration)

commit.message:
Change to tiered point system: Easy=1, Medium=3, Hard=6 pts

Instead of all-or-nothing 10 pts for perfect game:
- All 5 easy correct  → +1 pt
- All 5 medium correct → +3 pts
- All 5 hard correct  → +6 pts (max still 10)

End screen shows all 3 tiers with ✓ for completed levels
and the total earned for that session.

commit.message:
Add Intelligence tab: products, competitors, hot sources, keywords, uploaded followers

Server:
- GET/PUT /api/clients/:id/knowledge/:section (products|competitors|hot-sources|keywords|followers)
- Data stored as JSON in {clientDir}/knowledge/ — no new dependencies
- buildKnowledgeContext() injects brand intel into every Claude prompt:
  - Product names, prices, URLs, pain points, USPs
  - Competitor names/socials (never mention publicly)
  - Buying-signal and pain-point keywords (engage immediately)
  - Enabled hot sources (prioritise for outreach)

Admin UI — Intelligence tab in client edit view:
- Products: CRUD with name, price, URL, description, pain points, USPs, target keywords
- Competitors: table with website + platform handles + weaknesses + notes; add/edit/delete
- Hot Sources: type/platform/handle/why table with enabled toggle; add/delete
- Keywords: tag-cloud grouped by intent (commercial, transactional, pain-point, informational)
  with platform filter; color-coded by intent; add/delete
- Followers: CSV upload (browser-side parse → JSON POST); stats by platform; 200-row preview;
  Clear All; Download Template

commit.message:
Remove Machines input column — optimizer determines machine count

The number of machines per stage is the output of the optimizer, not a user
input. Removed the column from the data editor and templates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs: add GPU/NPU kernel fallback policy across Claude and Codex configs (#7)

Enforce that MPS/CUDA/NPU ops must never fall back to CPU. When a
native kernel has a bug, on-device composite workarounds are allowed
but the native kernel entry point must be preserved for future platform
upgrade testing. All known issues must be tracked in
docs/known-kernel-issues.md.

Changes:
- CLAUDE.md: expand "No Fallback" and "Kernel Implementation Priority"
- AGENTS.md: add "GPU/NPU Kernel Fallback Policy" mandatory section
- .codex/skills/gpu-kernel-fallback-policy/SKILL.md: Codex skill
- docs/known-kernel-issues.md: tracking document for kernel issues

- Schedule screen auto-selects game type and defaults from active team
- Team list and selector dropdown show (Boys)/(Girls) labels

Entire-Checkpoint: e7891b94ff05

* feat: implement ADR-004 penalty guidance for online score adjustments (#323)

Add guided penalty UI for organizers reviewing online competition video
submissions, following CrossFit's minor/major penalty framework as guidance.

Schema changes:
- Add penaltyType, penaltyPercentage, noRepCount columns to scoresTable
  (denormalized for leaderboard display)
- Add same columns to scoreVerificationLogsTable (audit trail)
- Add "invalid" as new verificationStatus and verification log action
- Add "invalid" to video submission reviewStatuses

Server function changes:
- Extend verifySubmissionScoreFn with "invalid" action (zeroes workout score)
- Add penaltyType/penaltyPercentage/noRepCount to adjust flow
- Set video submission reviewStatus to "penalized" when penalty applied
- Return penalty data from getSubmissionDetailFn and getVerificationLogsFn

UI changes:
- Add "Apply Penalty" flow with minor/major radio, percentage slider,
  no-rep count input, and live before/after score preview
- Add "Mark Invalid" action with confirmation dialog
- Show penalty info and invalid status on verification controls
- Display penalty details in audit log entries
- Add "invalid" status to submission-status-badge with Ban icon

matches of the 2026 World Cup. Includes group stage, best 3rd place
ranking, knockout bracket, and champion screen with local storage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add Zola docs validation to PR checks (#1396)

Broken internal anchor links in docs (like the `#command-approval` →
`#security` fix in #1391) were only caught post-merge by `publish-docs`.
This adds pre-merge validation and extends the auto-fix safety net.

**Changes:**
- New `check-docs` CI job runs `zola build` on PRs when `docs/**` or
`src/cli/mod.rs` changes, catching broken anchors before merge
- `claude-ci-fix` now watches `publish-docs` failures in addition to
`ci`, so docs build failures on main get auto-fixed
- `publish-docs` path filter extended to include `src/cli/mod.rs` (CLI
help text is the source of truth for docs content)

> _This was written by Claude Code on behalf of @max-sixty_

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
